zh-tw.title: 輕勵志語錄
zh-tw.description: 不說教、不灌雞湯，就是剛好戳中你的那種正能量短句。

en.title: Motivational Quotes
en.description: No preaching, no fluff — just the kind of honest encouragement that actually lands.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by common Taiwan motivational quote themes around starting before you're ready

zh-tw.content: 你不用準備好了才能開始，開始了才會準備好。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給猶豫要不要踏出第一步的人，這句話是最溫柔的推一把，不論是換工作、開始運動還是學新技能都適用。
variations: 完美的時機永遠不會自己出現，你要自己去創造它。

en.content: You don't have to be ready to start. You start, and then you get ready.
en.editorNote: Perfect for anyone stuck in the overthinking loop before a new goal. Send to a friend who keeps saying "I'll do it when I'm ready."
variations: The best time to start was yesterday. The second best time is right now.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by slow progress quotes from Taiwan productivity and wellness sites

zh-tw.content: 慢慢來，是前進，不是放棄。
zh-tw.editorNote: 壓力大又覺得進度太慢的時候，這句話能提醒你只要還在動就是贏，適合當桌面桌布或晨間提醒。
variations: 龜兔賽跑裡，龜還是跑到終點了。

en.content: Going slow is still going. And it still counts.
en.editorNote: Good for days when you feel like everyone else is sprinting while you're crawling. Slow and steady is underrated.
variations: Progress is progress, no matter the pace.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by rest and recharge themed quotes from CyberLink Taiwan blog compilation

zh-tw.content: 休息不是懶惰，是在充電準備繼續衝。
zh-tw.editorNote: 累了的時候給自己說，或傳給那個過勞還在撐的朋友。休息本來就是計畫的一部分。
variations: 連手機都要充電，你當然也需要。

en.content: Rest isn't quitting. It's loading the next level.
en.editorNote: Send to someone who needs permission to take a break but feels guilty about it. Works as a caption for a cozy day-off photo too.
variations: Even your phone needs charging. You're no different.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by failure reframe quotes from oxxostudio positive words collection

zh-tw.content: 失敗不是終點，只是說『這條路不對，試試別的吧』。
zh-tw.editorNote: 失敗後安慰自己或朋友時很好用，不說教也不假掰，就是幫你把失敗重新定義成資訊而不是結局。
variations: 你沒有失敗，你只是發現了一個不管用的方法。

en.content: Failing doesn't mean you lost. It means you found another path that doesn't work — cross it off and move on.
en.editorNote: Great for a post-setback pep talk. Reframes failure as data, not defeat, without sounding preachy.
variations: Every wrong turn still got you somewhere new.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by self-growth reflection quotes from Ariel Hsu 108 positive energy compilation

zh-tw.content: 你今天能做到的事，三個月前的你根本不敢想。
zh-tw.editorNote: 回顧自己成長最有感的句子，適合當月底或年末自我回顧時用，也適合在朋友達成小成就時傳過去。
variations: 現在覺得輕鬆的事，以前覺得是山。

en.content: The version of you from three months ago would be amazed by what you can do today.
en.editorNote: Best for end-of-month reflections or when someone achieves something they once thought was out of reach. A gentle reminder that growth is real.
variations: What's easy now was once impossible to you.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by journey and progress themed quotes from Manager Today's 39 curated quotes article

zh-tw.content: 不確定前方在哪，但你已經走了很遠了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 方向迷惘、不知道在幹嘛的時候最有感，提醒自己過去的努力是真實存在的，適合低潮期。
variations: 不知道終點在哪沒關係，你還在走就很厲害了。

en.content: You may not know where you're going, but look how far you've already come.
en.editorNote: For those feeling lost or directionless — shifts the focus from an unknown future to a very real past. Great for mid-journey slumps.
variations: The road ahead is uncertain. The miles behind you are not.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by anti-perfectionism themes from funny motivational quote compilations

zh-tw.content: 做得夠好，就是夠好了。不需要每次都完美。
zh-tw.editorNote: 完美主義者看了可能有點扎心，但真的很需要聽。適合在反覆修改、停不下來的時候提醒自己喊停。
variations: 完成，比完美更重要。

en.content: Good enough is genuinely enough. Not everything needs to be perfect.
en.editorNote: A permission slip for perfectionists. Especially helpful when you've been tweaking the same thing for hours and just need to hit send.
variations: Done is better than perfect. Always.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by comparison and social media-related wisdom from positivity blog collections

zh-tw.content: 你羨慕別人的時候，別人也在羨慕你某個部分。
zh-tw.editorNote: 刷社群媒體刷到不開心、覺得別人過得比你好的時候，這句話能幫你換個角度。比較遊戲沒有人真正贏。
variations: 每個人的精華都只放在社群上，你看到的從來不是全部。

en.content: While you're comparing yourself to them, they're probably comparing themselves to someone else.
en.editorNote: The perfect antidote to a social media comparison spiral. Nobody's winning the comparison game — it's a loop with no exit.
variations: Everyone's highlight reel looks better than your behind-the-scenes.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by small wins and incremental progress quotes from CMYK positive quotes collection

zh-tw.content: 今天比昨天更努力了一點點，這樣就夠了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 在能量很低、感覺什麼都做不了的日子，這句話提醒你小小的進步也算數，適合自我打氣或傳給正在低潮的朋友。
variations: 哪怕只是多喝了一杯水，今天也有在照顧自己。

en.content: If you tried a little harder than yesterday, that's already a win. No asterisks needed.
en.editorNote: For low-energy days when even getting out of bed felt like an achievement. Celebrate the micro-wins because they add up.
variations: Tiny steps still move you forward.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by anti-overwhelm and first-step themed quotes from Reader's Digest funny inspirational collection

zh-tw.content: 不用看清楚全部的路，踏出第一步就夠了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 面對大目標或複雜計畫時，這句話幫助分解焦慮，只需要聚焦在下一步，不需要全盤規劃完才行動。
variations: 你不需要地圖，只需要下一步。

en.content: You don't need to see the whole path. You just need to see the next step.
en.editorNote: Anti-overwhelm reminder for anyone frozen by a big goal. Focus shrinks the mountain to a manageable size.
variations: One step is enough to begin. The rest reveals itself as you go.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by behind-the-scenes of success themes from BuzzFeed funny motivational quotes compilation

zh-tw.content: 每個看起來很厲害的人，都有一段只有自己知道有多難的日子。
zh-tw.editorNote: 人生低谷期最有共鳴，提醒自己掙扎是成長的一部分，也提醒你不要只看別人的成功表面。
variations: 沒有人的精彩是從零代價開始的。

en.content: Every person who looks like they have it together has a chapter they don't talk about — the really hard one.
en.editorNote: A quiet comfort for rough patches. Reminds you that the polished version people show publicly is never the whole story.
variations: Behind every 'overnight success' is a very long night.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by fresh start and courage themes from Ariel Hsu 108 positive energy sentences

zh-tw.content: 重新開始不代表失敗，代表你夠勇敢選擇自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 換工作、換方向、走出一段感情後，這句話是給自己的溫柔肯定，重新出發本身就是一種勇氣。
variations: 敢說不再適合了，然後離開，才是真正認識自己。

en.content: Starting over isn't failure. It's choosing yourself again — and that takes guts.
en.editorNote: For any kind of fresh start: career change, moving cities, ending a relationship, pivoting a project. Reframing restarts as acts of courage, not defeat.
variations: Leaving what no longer fits is its own kind of bravery.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by courage and fear-despite-action themes from HuffPost funny inspirational quotes

zh-tw.content: 帶著害怕還是去做了，這才叫勇氣，不是無所畏懼。
zh-tw.editorNote: 即將踏出舒適圈前最需要的一句話，鼓勵有焦慮、有擔憂但還是決定嘗試的人。
variations: 你不需要不害怕，你只需要比害怕更想要那件事。

en.content: Courage isn't the absence of fear. It's being terrified and still going for it.
en.editorNote: Classic reframe, but it never gets old. Great for anyone about to take a scary leap — a job interview, a public presentation, a difficult conversation.
variations: Brave doesn't mean fearless. Brave means scared and doing it anyway.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by action-creating-motivation (not the other way around) themes from motivational quotes for work compilation

zh-tw.content: 你不需要有動力才開始，開始了動力自然就來。
zh-tw.editorNote: 打破「等我有動力再做」的拖延迴圈，先動起來再說，動力是行動的副產品，不是前提。
variations: 開始了就不想停，這是事實，不是雞湯。

en.content: You don't need to feel motivated to start. Start first, and motivation tends to follow.
en.editorNote: The counterintuitive truth about getting things done: waiting for motivation is a trap. Action comes first, feelings second.
variations: Motivation is a byproduct of starting, not a prerequisite.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by self-agency and personal empowerment themes from Inc's 365 quotes for 2026

zh-tw.content: 那個能讓你的生活變好的人，一直都是你自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 不是要你扛起所有責任，而是提醒你，你有比你想的更大的力量可以改變自己的處境，適合在想等別人來救自己的時候用。
variations: 不用等誰許可你過你想要的生活。

en.content: The person with the power to make your life better? You've been looking at them in the mirror every day.
en.editorNote: An empowering nudge to stop waiting for external rescue and start trusting your own agency. Not a call to do it all alone — just a reminder that you have more power than you think.
variations: Nobody's coming to save you. Luckily, you don't need saving — you need starting.
